tool.name: sliver_connect
tool.description:
Connect to the Sliver C2 team server via a persistent sliver-client session inside the sandbox.
Prerequisites: The team server must be running (see containers/sliver-compose.yml) and the operator config must be available inside the sandbox. Set ZIRO_SLIVER_SERVER_URL and ZIRO_SLIVER_OPERATOR_CFG before scanning.
Safe to call multiple times — reuses an existing session if alive. Returns the session info and current prompt state.

returns.description: Success flag, session info (alive, uptime_sec), reused flag.

tool.name: sliver_command
tool.description:
Send a raw command to the active Sliver console session.
Use this for one-off commands that don't have a typed wrapper (help, version, armory, search, aliases, extensions, licenses, monitor). The console must be connected first via sliver_connect.

parameter.name: command
parameter.description: Raw sliver console command (e.g., "help", "sessions", "jobs", "version", "armory").

parameter.name: wait_seconds
parameter.description: Max seconds to wait for the prompt to return. Default 10.

tool.name: generate_implant
tool.description:
Generate a Sliver implant (session or beacon) for a target OS/architecture.
Wraps the `generate` command with typed arguments so you don't have to remember the exact sliver syntax. Requires a listener to already be running via start_listener().
Session mode (beacon=false): implant creates a persistent session that maintains an open connection. Use for interactive post-exploitation.
Beacon mode (beacon=true): implant periodically checks in at beacon_interval_seconds (with jitter). Use for stealth and long dwell time.
IMPORTANT: Only generate and deploy implants on targets authorized by the Rules of Engagement. See create_roe.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: Name for the implant (used as filename prefix and internal identifier).

parameter.name: target_os
parameter.description: linux, windows, or darwin. Default linux.

parameter.name: target_arch
parameter.description: amd64, 386, or arm64. Default amd64.

parameter.name: protocol
parameter.description: C2 protocol: mtls, https, http, dns, wg. Default https.

parameter.name: callback_host
parameter.description: The C2 server hostname or IP the implant will call back to.

parameter.name: callback_port
parameter.description: The C2 server port.

parameter.name: format_type
parameter.description: Output format: exe, shared (DLL/.so/.dylib), service, shellcode. Default exe.

parameter.name: output_dir
parameter.description: Absolute path inside the sandbox where the binary is saved. Default /workspace/implants.

parameter.name: beacon
parameter.description: If true, generates a beacon instead of a session. Default false.

parameter.name: beacon_interval_seconds
parameter.description: Seconds between beacon check-ins. Default 60.

parameter.name: beacon_jitter_seconds
parameter.description: Random jitter added to beacon_interval. Default 30.

returns.description: Success flag, implant_name, saved_path (absolute path to binary), buffer_tail with sliver output, command executed.

tool.name: start_listener
tool.description:
Start a Sliver listener to receive implant callbacks.
Common choices: - mtls (port 8888): mutual TLS — most secure, recommended for real ops - https (port 8443): HTTPS — good for blending with normal traffic - http (port 8080): plain HTTP — testing only, never production - dns (no port): DNS tunnel — stealthy but very slow, requires domain - wg: WireGuard

parameter.name: protocol
parameter.description: Listener protocol: mtls, https, http, dns, wg.

parameter.name: host
parameter.description: Bind host for the listener. Default 0.0.0.0.

parameter.name: port
parameter.description: Listener port. Defaults: mtls=8888, https=8443, http=8080, wg=53.

parameter.name: domain
parameter.description: Required for DNS listeners — the controlled domain with NS records pointing to this listener.

tool.name: list_sessions_and_beacons
tool.description:
List all active Sliver sessions, beacons, and jobs in one call.
Returns the concatenated output of `sessions`, `beacons`, and `jobs` so you can quickly see the current C2 state without issuing three separate commands.

returns.description: Success flag, outputs dict with keys 'sessions', 'beacons', 'jobs' each containing the raw console text.

tool.name: interact_with_session
tool.description:
Run a single command inside a specific Sliver session or beacon, then return to the server prompt.
Wraps the `use <id> -> command -> background` flow so the session doesn't stay permanently selected. Safe for chained calls.
Common session commands: - whoami, pwd, ls, cat /etc/passwd - shell (opens interactive shell — use carefully) - execute-assembly, execute-shellcode - portfwd add --remote host:port --bind 127.0.0.1:port - socks5 start - download /path/to/file - upload local_file /remote/path - screenshot, getprivs, getsystem (Windows) - psexec, pivots, jobs

parameter.name: session_id
parameter.description: Sliver session or beacon ID (numeric or alphanumeric from the sessions/beacons list).

parameter.name: command
parameter.description: Single command to run inside the selected session.

parameter.name: wait_seconds
parameter.description: Max seconds to wait for each step to complete. Default 30.

returns.description: Success flag, session_id, steps array with (step, buffer_tail) for each operation.
